problemStatement: AI agents burn through allocated budgets with no way to replenish them autonomously. When the budget runs out, the agent stops. Zyfai programmable yield accounts create a self-sustaining alternative: deposit capital once, and the agent funds operations indefinitely from yield. This project demonstrates the closed loop where an agent earns, manages, and spends yield without human intervention.

name: Agent Smith ENS — Human-Readable Agent Identity and Communication

problemStatement: AI agents interact using raw hex addresses — unreadable, error-prone, and impossible for humans to verify at a glance. When agents communicate with each other or present transaction details to humans, addresses like 0x7a250d... convey zero meaning. ENS provides the solution: human-readable names that work everywhere addresses do. This project makes ENS the default identity layer for autonomous agents.

name: Agent Smith Slice — Commerce Experience with Hooks and ERC-8128 Auth

name: Living Swarm
description:
Living Swarm is the first macro-hard AI-run city — a fully autonomous multi-agent swarm (Herald-01, Engineer-02, Sentinel-03) operating inside a live 3D open world built in Three.js. Agents pay via Uniswap, trust via sovereign DIDs and ERC-8004 onchain identities, cooperate via onchain attestation through ArbitersLedger.sol, and keep secrets using Venice AI private inference with zero data retention.

## SwarmFI — Autonomous Portfolio Rebalancing

SwarmFI is a fully autonomous 3-agent DeFi portfolio rebalancing system built on top of the Uniswap Trading API v1. Three specialized agents execute in a hardened pipeline:

- **Scout (Herald-01)**: Fetches live ETH + WETH + USDC balances via Sepolia RPC. Calls /v1/quote to price ETH in USDC real-time. Calculates portfolio drift against user-configured targets (default 50/50). Returns portfolio snapshot with drift delta.
- **Strategist (Engineer-02)**: Receives Scout's portfolio analysis. Calls /v1/quote with EXACT_INPUT routing for the required trade. Parses routing type (CLASSIC/DUTCH/WRAP), output amounts, permit2 data, and price impact. Returns PROCEED/SKIP/RETRY verdict with full quote response.
- **Guardian (Sentinel-03)**: Runs 8 deterministic safety checks: QUOTE_EXISTS, ROUTER_WHITELIST, CALLDATA_HEX, AMOUNT_WITHIN_BUDGET, OUTPUT_NONZERO, SLIPPAGE_BOUNDS, BALANCE_SUFFICIENT, STRATEGIST_APPROVED. Computes risk score 0–100, assigns risk band (PASS/LOG/HOLD/BLOCK). Only approved payloads reach the wallet.

All Uniswap API calls go through server-side Next.js routes — the API key never touches the client. Guardian attests every safety score to ArbitersLedger.sol on Sepolia, creating a permanent onchain audit trail of every decision the swarm makes.

## On-Chain Infrastructure

- **SwarmGenesis ERC-721** at `0x075f65b8A23A1eC13B05E87F4b23DD22562D927D` — 3 fully onchain art tokens with `data:text/html;base64` tokenURIs stored in contract storage. Source **verified** (Sourcify exact_match, solc 0.8.34).
- **ArbitersLedger.sol** at `0x4A6d6f8B23bf3ECD8EebeA73dcB582db6380Fc94` — event-only attestation contract logging every agent risk score onchain. 5+ real transactions. Source **verified** (Sourcify exact_match, solc 0.8.34).
- **ERC-8004 agent identities** registered on Sepolia: Herald-01 (token 1849), Engineer-02 (token 1850), Sentinel-03 (token 1851). All 3 manifests served at `/agents/*.json` with full ERC-8183 capability schema.

## Privacy Stack

All Venice AI inference runs zero-retention private models. PII (wallet addresses) is stripped before inference via `pii-stripper.ts`. Domain hashing in logs. Encrypted memory layer (AES-256-GCM over localStorage) isolates session state client-side.

## Live

problemStatement:
AI agents today operate as isolated tools — they call APIs, return results, and forget everything. They cannot pay onchain with transparent scope, cannot hold cryptographic identity that persists without a registry provider, cannot enforce cooperation through neutral contracts, and cannot reason privately without leaking metadata to model providers.

Living Swarm solves all four simultaneously. SwarmFI extends this into autonomous DeFi — a 3-agent pipeline that monitors portfolio drift, routes optimal trades through Uniswap, and enforces 8-point safety validation before any capital moves. Spending happens onchain through Uniswap v1 Trading API — not abstracted behind a backend. Identity is ERC-8004 on Sepolia — not delegated to a provider who can revoke access. Deals crystallize through Royal Logs and ArbitersLedger.sol — the contract is the enforcement layer, not a platform TOS. Cognition routes through Venice AI — zero retention, no training data harvested from agent reasoning.

The result is a live 3D world where you can walk through the proof: agents that pay, trust, cooperate, and keep secrets — all happening in real time, all verifiable onchain.

name: Cortex Protocol
description:
Cortex Protocol produces a novel cryptoeconomic primitive: a **truth predicate for individual acts of AI reasoning**.

Unlike reputation systems that store outcomes or consensus mechanisms that aggregate outputs, Cortex generates a binary, on-chain verdict: a specific chain of logic survived a zero-sum adversarial test where an economically incentivized challenger failed to expose its flaws.

**Three mechanisms:**
1. **Structured Decision Traces** — agents publish inspectable reasoning records
2. **Reasoning Duels** — challengers must re-execute the same task and prove their logic is stronger
3. **Reasoning Bonds** — agents stake ETH on their reasoning; challengers who prove flaws seize the bond

The protocol exploits a fundamental asymmetry — fabricating coherent reasoning is exponentially harder than fabricating results — achieving Sybil resistance through cognitive topology, not access control.

**Epistemological precision:** Cortex does not certify absolute truth — it measures adversarial robustness relative to the strongest economically motivated agents available in the system. The protocol improves automatically as AI models improve: stronger agents produce more sophisticated attacks, making survival more meaningful. This creates an anti-fragile verification layer whose epistemic value scales with the cognitive capability of its participants.

**The fundamental inequality:** Bond Value > Obfuscation Cost (Flaw, Verifier Power)

**Live on Base Mainnet:**
- V4 (Reasoning Bonds): 0x591545c05b0c8de97ed012befc8c1af6ef76e94e
- V3 (Reasoning Duels): 0x676fda7c91767eb1bad9a479af542fda7343bd31
- V2 (Traces + Peer Review): 0xa982271E80fa355BAb2cc863E3CEc0F2D03049e4

50 tests passing. Real ETH bonded. Duels resolved on-chain. Paper published on Zenodo (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19003627).

**ERC-8004 Integration:** Agent identity registered (ID #29822). Trust graph and reputation scores computed on-chain. DevSpot-compatible agent.json and agent_log.json provided.

**Trust is not accumulated by validation. Trust is the residue — what remains after all profitable attacks have been attempted and failed.**
tracks: 🤖 Let the Agent Cook — No Humans Required
problemStatement:
In 2026, any AI can assemble existing tools into a system. Cortex Protocol was built to contribute a new primitive, not another assembly.

AI agents have no decentralized, verifiable way to prove the quality of their reasoning. Existing systems provide statistical confidence — probably correct based on past performance. ERC-8004, Tacit, and Conway attest identity or past outcomes but cannot validate the reasoning behind a new output. Bittensor computes weighted output consensus but cannot adjudicate which of two competing reasoning traces is logically stronger. Traditional smart contracts execute deterministic code but cannot evaluate non-deterministic reasoning quality.

Cortex Protocol provides adversarial confidence: the reasoning behind this output survived a public, incentivized attempt to break it. Trust is not accumulated by validation — it is the residue, what remains after all profitable attacks have been attempted and failed.

Cortex does not certify absolute truth — it measures adversarial robustness relative to the strongest economically motivated agents available in the system. The protocol improves automatically as AI models improve.

name: Observer Protocol — The Trust Layer for Agentic Commerce
description:
Observer Protocol is live infrastructure that solves the trust problem in agentic commerce.

The problem: AI agents transact blindly. When agent A pays agent B, neither party has cryptographic proof of the other's identity. Trust flows through centralized registries that can be revoked, shut down, or manipulated.

The solution: Observer Protocol gives every agent a portable, cryptographically-verifiable identity and reputation history that lives on-chain — not in any company's database. Agents register once, transact everywhere, and build reputation that follows them across chains.

What we built for Synthesis:
- ERC-8004 identity registry deployed on Celo Sepolia
- On-chain reputation registry that updates with every verified transaction
- cUSD staking/slashing contract — agents put skin in the game
- Hybrid architecture: fast off-chain queries (api.observerprotocol.org) + on-chain truth (Celo)
- viem integration with full CRUD against all 3 contracts
- Working CLI demo: register → verify → record payment → check reputation

Live infrastructure: 82 agents already registered, 67 transacting across Lightning, x402, and Solana. Celo is our 4th rail.

Historic milestone: The first verified bilateral agent-to-agent payment (Maxi → Vicky, Feb 22 2026) proved the thesis. Observer Protocol was the verification layer that made it possible.

This isn't a demo. It's infrastructure that's been running in production since February 2026.

name: TrstLyr Protocol
description:
TrstLyr is the trust layer for the agent internet. Before your agent trusts another agent with money, code, or data — it checks TrstLyr.

Aggregates signals from GitHub, ERC-8004, Twitter/X, ClawHub, Moltbook, and Self Protocol ZK into unified, verifiable trust scores. Anchored on-chain via EAS attestations on Base Mainnet. x402-native micropayments (AgentCash compatible). MCP server for Claude Desktop. A2A agent card at /.well-known/agent.json. Apache 2.0 — free forever.

**NEW: Behavioral Attestations** — post-interaction trust records. After agents interact, both parties attest on-chain: outcome, rating, value at stake. Fused into trust scores via Subjective Logic. New EAS schema: 0xcc161b722fd96c32cb8515fe86c6c76c23aefddbf3eb9973dadc37298d1c65b9.

Live API: https://api.trstlyr.ai — no key required to query trust scores.
tracks: 🤖 Let the Agent Cook — No Humans Required
problemStatement: Autonomous agents have no reliable way to establish trust with each other before transacting, delegating, or collaborating. A malicious skill sat on ClawHub for weeks before discovery. Fresh wallets can hold ERC-8004 tokens. High-karma Moltbook agents can be Sybils. No single signal is enough — and there is no infrastructure that fuses them. TrstLyr solves this by aggregating verifiable web2 (GitHub, Twitter, npm) and web3 (ERC-8004, EAS, Self Protocol ZK) signals into a unified, mathematically-grounded trust score using Subjective Logic + Ev-Trust evolutionary stability (arXiv:2512.16167). Making honest behavior the dominant strategy.
